title: bridge_health.sh → bridge_health_history.tsv per-bridge wiring + opt-in CLI-only anomaly auto-invocation
summary: Close the omega-bridge-14 deferred wiring. Modify tool/bridge_health.sh (grandfathered .sh, raw 9 exception) to APPEND a per-bridge per-sweep row (ts<TAB>bridge<TAB>status<TAB>duration_ms<TAB>ec) to state/bridge_health_history.tsv. Three call sites covered: script_missing (FAIL/0/NA), TAMPERED (TAMPERED/0/NA), and post-selftest (STATUS/DUR_ms/EC). Add opt-in --anomaly-check / -A flag mirroring the existing --fingerprint-check / -F pattern; when set, after sweep the script invokes `hexa run tool/bridge_anomaly.hexa --quiet --history $BRIDGE_HISTORY_TSV` via the canonical hexa CLI binary (no library import, no path coupling). Add --no-history escape hatch for tests. Validation: bash -n syntax pass; analyzer dry-run on 9-row fixture (5 codata samples with 2500ms spike at tail + 4 oeis samples ending FAIL) emits __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ WARN bridges=2 samples=9 flagged=2.

predecessors.omega_bridge_14: analyzer + history TSV schema stub landed (deferred wiring)
predecessors.omega_bridge_7: established opt-in --fingerprint-check flag pattern reused here for --anomaly-check

context.problem: Analyzer landed in omega-bridge-14 but read-only — without bridge_health.sh appending per-bridge rows the canonical default path always returned INSUFFICIENT_DATA. No automated detection feedback loop existed.
context.design_goal: Minimal-blast wiring: raw 77 append-only TSV + opt-in CLI auto-invoke. No mandatory analyzer cost on every sweep (preserves cron speed); no library coupling between .sh runner and .hexa analyzer (CLI-only inter-call constraint).

additions: append_history_row() helper — printf-only, no exec fan-out
3 call sites: script_missing branch, TAMPERED branch, post-selftest end-of-iteration
post-summary anomaly invocation block — HEXA_ARGV='--quiet --history ...' hexa run bridge_anomaly.hexa, non-fatal on failure (raw 71)
files_edited.help_block_extension: sed -n '3,44p' bumped to '3,47p' to absorb 3 new usage lines

subcommands_added.--anomaly-check (-A): opt-in: after sweep, invoke bridge_anomaly.hexa via $HEXA_BIN run (CLI-only, no library import)
subcommands_added.--no-history: opt-out: skip per-bridge TSV append (testing/dry-run path)

data_path.atomicity: single printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' is shell-builtin → no exec fan-out, line-buffered append is atomic for the row sizes here (< PIPE_BUF / 4096)

cli_only_invocation_proof.binding: no .hexa import, no path-based stdlib reuse
cli_only_invocation_proof.transport: argv (HEXA_ARGV env) + stdout (sentinel line) + exit code (non-fatal swallow)

duration_unit_compromise.issue: bridge_health.sh uses date +%s for T0/T1 → integer-second granularity. Schema is duration_ms.
duration_unit_compromise.decision: Multiply DUR * 1000 to satisfy schema. Sub-second timing is lossy but consistent with all existing per-bridge wall measurements in bridge_health.sh.
duration_unit_compromise.future_refinement: Switch to date +%s%3N (gnu date) or perl -MTime::HiRes when sub-second comparison becomes load-bearing. Deferred — current spike threshold (3× over 200ms floor) tolerates the ±999ms quantisation.

validation.stdout: __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ WARN bridges=2 samples=9 flagged=2 window=7 mult=3.0
validation.interpretation: codata DURATION_SPIKE (150ms→2500ms = 16×) + oeis FRESH_FAILURE (PASS-dominant prior, FAIL tail) — both rules fired correctly

validation.step: exact bridge_health.sh invocation simulated via env-var passthrough
validation.result: identical sentinel output → wiring path proven

scope_constraints_honoured: raw 71 report-only (analyzer auto-invoke is non-fatal on failure)
no parallel-session collision (kick_* untracked files NOT touched; only tracked bridge_health.sh edited)
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked

non_changes.atlas_health_timeline.jsonl: currently dirty (parallel-session modification) — NOT touched by this cycle

next_followups: data accumulation: run bridge_health.sh -A in cron for 7+ sweeps to populate the rolling window per bridge
if duration-spike rule becomes noisy at second-granularity → upgrade to %s%3N timing
consider exposing __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ in atlas_health_timeline.jsonl as a sibling JSONL line (separate cycle if pursued)
